children.value: This page explains what each command family is for before listing concrete selectors.
Devflow commands are categorized by their role in the developer lifecycle.
Check if code matches project formatting standards
Run clippy, eslint, or other static analyzers
Clean up stale GHA workflow runs
Build and bundle project distribution artifacts
Tag and prepare a new release candidate
Devflow provides Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform) for managing GitHub repository settings.
and generates a plan for repository settings, branch protection, and security policies.
The project includes standard GitHub community files in the root and
: Structured templates for Bugs, Features, and Security Incidents.
target for a “scorched earth” local reset.
Use this when you need to completely refresh the container engine state or clear up major disk space occupied by untagged images.
This command is used to reclaim disk space or reset
state. It supports granular target selection via flags.
If total GH storage exceeds 8GB, it performs “LIFO” pruning on cargo caches (keeps the latest for each ref).
Cleans up the GitHub Actions execution history. Requires
: custom stack config for Make/Just-backed Kotlin workflows
: runs any custom profile key defined under
: validate on-disk workflow topology and detect drift
: list configured profile keys used by

dwf fmt:check
dwf lint:static
dwf test:unit
dwf check:pr
